todaysPlan.activity: Homework Spelling – Silent Letter Spelling Patterns And High-frequency Word Recognition
todaysPlan.reason: Active uploaded homework is "Benchmark Advance Spelling Unit 9 Week 3", so today's required work must anchor on Silent consonants in English orthography, High-frequency word recognition, Pattern-based spelling versus whole-word memory before broader profile goals.
todaysPlan.method: Read the assignment-aligned content, ask evidence-based checks, then use profile motivators only as framing.
probeSequence: Read or preview the assignment content
Ask why it happened using text evidence
Have the child explain the concept back

childProfile: Reina is an 8-year-old academically advanced student who thrives on challenge, competition, and being pushed intellectually. She's a year ahead of grade level, scores 100% on spelling tests, and gets bored—not frustrated—when work is too easy. As a competitive wrestler, she responds powerfully to wrestling metaphors (takedowns, pins, championships), scoreboard tracking, and personal-best challenges. Bilingual in English and Japanese, she celebrates wins in both languages (すごい, やった) and wants her Japanese identity acknowledged. She loves the movie Matilda and sees herself in the smart girl who doesn't back down. Reina is fast-paced, sharp, catches errors instantly (debugs technical glitches analytically), and self-regulates well—she'll exit on a winning streak or ask to stop when fatigued (15-20 minute focus window). She explicitly requested multiplication and division work 79 days ago and deserves that challenge immediately. She's not here for baby talk or slow pacing—she's here to be seen as the smart, competitive champion she is.
stopAfter: After the active homework check is complete, or earlier if frustration is rising.
rewardPolicy: Reward only after the active homework is checked; broader review goals belong in review mode.
